child.name: resourceType
child.description: This is a ValueSet resource

child.name: id
child.description: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.

child.name: meta
child.description: The metadata about the resource. This is content that is maintained by the infrastructure. Changes to the content might not alwa...
child.longDesc: The metadata about the resource. This is content that is maintained by the infrastructure. Changes to the content might not always be associated with version changes to the resource.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: versionId
child.description: The version specific identifier, as it appears in the version portion of the URL. This value changes when the resource is create...
child.longDesc: The version specific identifier, as it appears in the version portion of the URL. This value changes when the resource is created, updated, or deleted.

child.name: lastUpdated
child.description: When the resource last changed - e.g. when the version changed.

child.name: source
child.description: A uri that identifies the source system of the resource. This provides a minimal amount of [[[Provenance]]] information that can...
child.longDesc: A uri that identifies the source system of the resource. This provides a minimal amount of [[[Provenance]]] information that can be used to track or differentiate the source of information in the resource. The source may identify another FHIR server, document, message, database, etc.

child.name: profile
child.description: A list of profiles (references to [[[StructureDefinition]]] resources) that this resource claims to conform to. The URL is a ref...
child.longDesc: A list of profiles (references to [[[StructureDefinition]]] resources) that this resource claims to conform to. The URL is a reference to [[[StructureDefinition.url]]].

child.name: security
child.description: Security labels applied to this resource. These tags connect specific resources to the overall security policy and infrastructur...
child.longDesc: Security labels applied to this resource. These tags connect specific resources to the overall security policy and infrastructure.

child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: tag
child.description: Tags applied to this resource. Tags are intended to be used to identify and relate resources to process and workflow, and applic...
child.longDesc: Tags applied to this resource. Tags are intended to be used to identify and relate resources to process and workflow, and applications are not required to consider the tags when interpreting the meaning of a resource.

child.name: implicitRules
child.description: A reference to a set of rules that were followed when the resource was constructed, and which must be understood when processing...
child.longDesc: A reference to a set of rules that were followed when the resource was constructed, and which must be understood when processing the content. Often, this is a reference to an implementation guide that defines the special rules along with other profiles etc.

child.name: language
child.description: The base language in which the resource is written.

child.name: text
child.description: A human-readable narrative that contains a summary of the resource and can be used to represent the content of the resource to a...
child.longDesc: A human-readable narrative that contains a summary of the resource and can be used to represent the content of the resource to a human. The narrative need not encode all the structured data, but is required to contain sufficient detail to make it "clinically safe" for a human to just read the narrative. Resource definitions may define what content should be represented in the narrative to ensure clinical safety.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: status
child.description: The status of the narrative - whether it's entirely generated (from just the defined data or the extensions too), or whether a h...
child.longDesc: The status of the narrative - whether it's entirely generated (from just the defined data or the extensions too), or whether a human authored it and it may contain additional data.

child.name: div
child.description: The actual narrative content, a stripped down version of XHTML.

child.name: url
child.description: An absolute URI that is used to identify this value set when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance; ...
child.longDesc: An absolute URI that is used to identify this value set when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance; also called its canonical identifier. This SHOULD be globally unique and SHOULD be a literal address at which an authoritative instance of this value set is (or will be) published. This URL can be the target of a canonical reference. It SHALL remain the same when the value set is stored on different servers.

child.description: Establishes the namespace for the value - that is, an absolute URL that describes a set values that are unique.

child.name: value
child.description: The portion of the identifier typically relevant to the user and which is unique within the context of the system.

child.name: period
child.description: Time period during which identifier is/was valid for use.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: start
child.description: The start of the period. The boundary is inclusive.

child.name: end
child.description: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was cr...
child.longDesc: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was created. The start may be in the past, and the end date in the future, which means that period is expected/planned to end at that time.

child.name: assigner
child.description: Organization that issued/manages the identifier.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: reference
child.description: A reference to a location at which the other resource is found. The reference may be a relative reference, in which case it is r...
child.longDesc: A reference to a location at which the other resource is found. The reference may be a relative reference, in which case it is relative to the service base URL, or an absolute URL that resolves to the location where the resource is found. The reference may be version specific or not. If the reference is not to a FHIR RESTful server, then it should be assumed to be version specific. Internal fragment references (start with '#') refer to contained resources.

child.name: type
child.description: The expected type of the target of the reference. If both Reference.type and Reference.reference are populated and Reference.ref...
child.longDesc:
The expected type of the target of the reference. If both Reference.type and Reference.reference are populated and Reference.reference is a FHIR URL, both SHALL be consistent.
The type is the Canonical URL of Resource Definition that is the type this reference refers to. References are URLs that are relative to http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/ e.g. "Patient" is a reference to http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Patient. Absolute URLs are only allowed for logical models (and can only be used in references in logical models, not resources).

child.name: identifier
child.description: An identifier for the target resource. This is used when there is no way to reference the other resource directly, either becaus...
child.longDesc: An identifier for the target resource. This is used when there is no way to reference the other resource directly, either because the entity it represents is not available through a FHIR server, or because there is no way for the author of the resource to convert a known identifier to an actual location. There is no requirement that a Reference.identifier point to something that is actually exposed as a FHIR instance, but it SHALL point to a business concept that would be expected to be exposed as a FHIR instance, and that instance would need to be of a FHIR resource type allowed by the reference.

child.name: display
child.description: Plain text narrative that identifies the resource in addition to the resource reference.

child.name: version
child.description: The identifier that is used to identify this version of the value set when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or...
child.longDesc: The identifier that is used to identify this version of the value set when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or instance. This is an arbitrary value managed by the value set author and is not expected to be globally unique. For example, it might be a timestamp (e.g. yyyymmdd) if a managed version is not available. There is also no expectation that versions can be placed in a lexicographical sequence.

child.name: versionAlgorithmString
child.description: Indicates the mechanism used to compare versions to determine which ValueSet is more current.

child.name: versionAlgorithmCoding
child.description: Indicates the mechanism used to compare versions to determine which ValueSet is more current.

child.longDesc: A natural language name identifying the value set. This name should be usable as an identifier for the module by machine processing applications such as code generation.

child.name: title
child.description: A short, descriptive, user-friendly title for the value set.

child.name: status
child.description: The status of this value set. Enables tracking the life-cycle of the content. The status of the value set applies to the value s...
child.longDesc: The status of this value set. Enables tracking the life-cycle of the content. The status of the value set applies to the value set definition (ValueSet.compose) and the associated ValueSet metadata. Expansions do not have a state.

child.name: experimental
child.description: A Boolean value to indicate that this value set is authored for testing purposes (or education/evaluation/marketing) and is not ...
child.longDesc: A Boolean value to indicate that this value set is authored for testing purposes (or education/evaluation/marketing) and is not intended to be used for genuine usage.

child.description: The date (and optionally time) when the value set metadata or content logical definition (.compose) was created or revised.

child.description: The text to display to the user for this concept in the context of this valueset. If no display is provided, then applications u...
child.longDesc: The text to display to the user for this concept in the context of this valueset. If no display is provided, then applications using the value set use the display specified for the code by the system.

child.description: Additional representations for this concept when used in this value set - other languages, aliases, specialized purposes, used f...
child.longDesc: Additional representations for this concept when used in this value set - other languages, aliases, specialized purposes, used for particular purposes, etc.

child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.description: Additional codes that detail how this designation would be used, if there is more than one use.

child.name: filter
child.description: Select concepts by specifying a matching criterion based on the properties (including relationships) defined by the system, or o...
child.longDesc: Select concepts by specifying a matching criterion based on the properties (including relationships) defined by the system, or on filters defined by the system. If multiple filters are specified within the include, they SHALL all be true.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: property
child.description: A code that identifies a property or a filter defined in the code system.

child.name: op
child.description: The kind of operation to perform as a part of the filter criteria.

child.name: value
child.description: The match value may be either a code defined by the system, or a string value, which is a regex match on the literal string of t...
child.longDesc: The match value may be either a code defined by the system, or a string value, which is a regex match on the literal string of the property value (if the filter represents a property defined in CodeSystem) or of the system filter value (if the filter represents a filter defined in CodeSystem) when the operation is 'regex', or one of the values (true and false), when the operation is 'exists'.

child.name: valueSet
child.description: Selects the concepts found in this value set (based on its value set definition). This is an absolute URI that is a reference to...
child.longDesc: Selects the concepts found in this value set (based on its value set definition). This is an absolute URI that is a reference to ValueSet.url. If multiple value sets are specified this includes the intersection of the contents of all of the referenced value sets.

child.name: copyright
child.description: A copyright statement for the specific code system asserted by the containing ValueSet.compose.include element's system value (i...
child.longDesc: A copyright statement for the specific code system asserted by the containing ValueSet.compose.include element's system value (if the associated ValueSet.compose.include.version element is not present); or the code system and version combination (if the associated ValueSet.compose.include.version element is present).

child.name: property
child.description: A property to return in the expansion, if the client doesn't ask for any particular properties. May be either a code from the co...
child.longDesc: A property to return in the expansion, if the client doesn't ask for any particular properties. May be either a code from the code system definition (convenient) or a the formal URI that refers to the property. The special value '*' means all properties known to the server.

child.name: expansion
child.description: A value set can also be "expanded", where the value set is turned into a simple collection of enumerated codes. This element hol...
child.longDesc: A value set can also be "expanded", where the value set is turned into a simple collection of enumerated codes. This element holds the expansion, if it has been performed.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: identifier
child.description: An identifier that uniquely identifies this expansion of the valueset, based on a unique combination of the provided parameters,...
child.longDesc: An identifier that uniquely identifies this expansion of the valueset, based on a unique combination of the provided parameters, the system default parameters, and the underlying system code system versions etc. Systems may re-use the same identifier as long as those factors remain the same, and the expansion is the same, but are not required to do so. This is a business identifier.

child.name: next
child.description: As per paging Search results, the next URLs are opaque to the client, have no dictated structure, and only the server understand...
child.longDesc: As per paging Search results, the next URLs are opaque to the client, have no dictated structure, and only the server understands them.

child.name: timestamp
child.description: The time at which the expansion was produced by the expanding system.

child.name: total
child.description: The total number of concepts in the expansion. If the number of concept nodes in this resource is less than the stated number, t...
child.longDesc: The total number of concepts in the expansion. If the number of concept nodes in this resource is less than the stated number, then the server can return more using the offset parameter.

child.name: offset
child.description: If paging is being used, the offset at which this resource starts. I.e. this resource is a partial view into the expansion. If ...
child.longDesc: If paging is being used, the offset at which this resource starts. I.e. this resource is a partial view into the expansion. If paging is not being used, this element SHALL NOT be present.

child.name: parameter
child.description: A parameter that controlled the expansion process. These parameters may be used by users of expanded value sets to check whether...
child.longDesc: A parameter that controlled the expansion process. These parameters may be used by users of expanded value sets to check whether the expansion is suitable for a particular purpose, or to pick the correct expansion.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: name
child.description: Name of the input parameter to the $expand operation; may be a server-assigned name for additional default or other server-suppl...
child.longDesc: Name of the input parameter to the $expand operation; may be a server-assigned name for additional default or other server-supplied parameters used to control the expansion process.

child.name: valueString
child.description: The value of the parameter.

child.name: valueBoolean
child.description: The value of the parameter.

child.name: abstract
child.description: If true, this entry is included in the expansion for navigational purposes, and the user cannot select the code directly as a pr...
child.longDesc: If true, this entry is included in the expansion for navigational purposes, and the user cannot select the code directly as a proper value.

child.name: inactive
child.description: If the concept is inactive in the code system that defines it. Inactive codes are those that are no longer to be used, but are m...
child.longDesc: If the concept is inactive in the code system that defines it. Inactive codes are those that are no longer to be used, but are maintained by the code system for understanding legacy data. It might not be known or specified whether a concept is inactive (and it may depend on the context of use).

child.name: version

child.longDesc: The version of the code system from this code was taken. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.
